flashcards.back: The automated process of assigning predefined categories or tags to unstructured text based on its content.

flashcards.front: State the formula for Bayes' Theorem as used in Naive Bayes classification.
flashcards.back: P(C|D) ∝ P(C) * P(w1|C) * P(w2|C) * ... * P(wn|C), where C is the class and D is the document represented by words w1 to wn.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Topic Labeling and Sentiment Analysis?
flashcards.back: Topic Labeling categorizes text by its subject matter (e.g., sports, politics). Sentiment Analysis categorizes text by the opinion or emotion it expresses (e.g., positive, negative).
Precision: When the model predicts positive, how often is it correct? (TP/(TP+FP)). Recall: How many of the actual positives did the model find? (TP/(TP+FN)).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the Bag-of-Words (BoW) model?
flashcards.back: To represent a text document as an unordered multiset (bag) of its words, disregarding grammar and word order but keeping track of word frequency. It is a simple feature extraction technique.
